02 What we do

Long-term owners.
Hands-on operators.

We acquire profitable apps and SaaS and run them ourselves. Not consultants. Not a rollup fund. We're operators who've actually built, scaled, and sold software. Now we're buying the kinds of products we used to build.

03 Why founders choose us

A home for what
you've built.

If you care about your product and the people who use it, you want a buyer who does too. Here is what that actually looks like with us.

48Hours

First real reply, from a real person.

Rob or Liam reads every inbound. No intake form, no associate filter, no "we'll circle back after the IC meeting."

100Percent

Your brand, your product, your name.

We do not rebrand. We do not consolidate. The logo your customers have spent years learning to trust stays on the product, and the team stays in place unless they choose otherwise.

10+Years

We hold. We do not flip.

We acquire to hold, not to flip. We're building the firm we wished existed when we were selling Time Doctor.

04 What we look for

Apps and SaaS with
something real underneath.

We partner with founders who have built a product that already works profitable or close to it, with users who keep coming back. The kind of business a founder has put real care into, ready for its next chapter.

Outside that range? Still send a note. We will tell you honestly if it is a fit.

See the full criteria
Annual recurring revenue
$500K to $2M Profitable, or nearly there
05 Who is behind this

A firm,
made for you.

Operators who have shipped, scaled, and sold software themselves. These are the two people you'll actually be talking to.

Co-founder

Rob Rawson

Co-founded Time Doctor in 2011 and bootstrapped it to an eight-figure SaaS with a distributed team of 130+ people across 31 countries. Co-organizer of the Running Remote Conference. Former medical doctor.

Sydney, Australia
Co-founder

Liam Martin

Co-founder of Time Doctor. Co-founder of Running Remote, the largest remote work conference in the world. Co-author of Running Remote (HarperCollins, 2022), a Wall Street Journal bestseller.

Toronto, Canada
